title: Financial Mathematics
blurb: Compound growth and decay, nominal and effective rates, annuities, loans, outstanding balances and sinking-fund style timelines.

questions.context: A delivery bakkie is bought for R380 000. Its value depreciates at $14\%$ per annum on the reducing-balance method. The price of a new bakkie of the same model increases with inflation at $6\%$ per annum.

parts.q: Calculate the value of the bakkie after $5$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the price of a NEW bakkie of the same model after $5$ years. Round to the nearest cent.

questions.context: Bank A offers an interest rate of $10{,}5\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. Bank B offers $10{,}8\%$ per annum, compounded annually.

parts.q: Convert Bank A's rate to an effective annual interest rate. Give your answer correct to TWO decimal places.
Which bank offers the better rate for an investor? Justify your answer.
solution: Bank A's effective rate is $11{,}02\%$, Bank B's effective rate is $10{,}8\%$
$11{,}02\%>10{,}8\%$, so Bank A is better for an investor
parts.answer: Bank A, since its effective rate ($11{,}02\%$) exceeds Bank B's $10{,}8\%$

questions.context: Thandi wants to save for a deposit on a flat. She pays R1 500 into a savings account at the END of every month for $6$ years. The account earns interest at $9\%$ per annum, compounded monthly.

parts.q: Calculate the value of Thandi's savings at the end of the $6$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
How much interest does Thandi earn over the $6$ years?

questions.context: The Dlamini family takes out a home loan of R950 000 to buy a house in Soweto. Interest is charged at $11\%$ per annum, compounded monthly, and the loan is repaid with equal monthly payments over $20$ years, starting one month after the loan is granted.

parts.q: Calculate the monthly payment. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the outstanding balance of the loan immediately after the $96$th payment (i.e. after $8$ years). Round to the nearest cent.
solution: $=$ R$782\ 238{,}29$ (nearest cent, using the rounded payment of R$9\ 805{,}79$; using the unrounded payment gives R$782\ 238{,}33$)
parts.answer: R782 238,29 (accept small differences from rounding the monthly payment)

parts.q: Calculate the TOTAL interest paid over the full $20$ years if all $240$ payments are made as scheduled.

questions.context: Sipho opens a savings account with a single deposit of R20 000. From the end of the first month he also deposits R500 at the END of every month for $5$ years. The account earns $8\%$ per annum, compounded monthly. Exactly $3$ years after opening the account he withdraws R10 000 to repair his taxi.

parts.q: Calculate the value that the initial R20 000 deposit alone grows to at the end of the $5$ years. Round to the nearest cent.
Calculate the balance in the account at the end of the $5$ years, taking ALL the transactions into account. Round to the nearest cent.
solution: The R$10\ 000$ withdrawal accumulates interest against the account for the remaining $2$ years ($24$ months): $10\ 000\left(1+\dfrac{0{,}08}{12}\right)^{24}=$ R$11\ 728{,}88$
